general.text: To design, implement, and evaluate an integrated, privacy-preserving, blockchain-based attendance verification system that (a) utilizes {cmhb|cancelable multimodal-hierarchical biometrics} and a {didvc|user-centric DID/VC framework} to enforce secure {sync|credential lifecycle synchronization}, and (b) maintains auditable and verifiable attendance records across a distributed network of {edge|physically accessible edge validators}.

keywords.label: Cancelable multimodal-hierarchical biometrics
keywords.definition: Uses multiple biometric traits (e.g. face + fingerprint) processed through irreversible transforms so they can be revoked without exposing raw data.

keywords.label: DID/VC framework
keywords.definition: Decentralized Identifiers and Verifiable Credentials; a W3C standard enabling users to hold and present identity proofs without a central authority.

keywords.label: Credential lifecycle synchronization
keywords.definition: Keeping enrollment, verification, re-issuance, and revocation states consistent across all nodes in real time.

keywords.label: Edge validators
keywords.definition: Blockchain nodes co-located with biometric capture devices (e.g. school gate kiosks) that validate transactions locally.

specific.short: Build & evaluate multimodal biometric accuracy using cancelable templates
specific.detail: Develop a multimodal-hierarchical biometrics-based attendance system that employs cancelable transformation techniques and evaluate its recognition accuracy using solely the transformed templates, never exposing raw biometric data.

specific.short: Integrate biometric layer with blockchain for immutable, auditable records
specific.detail: Integrate the biometric layer with a blockchain network for anchoring transformed templates, and evaluate the immutability, auditability, and consistency of records anchored by physically accessible edge nodes.

specific.short: Implement DID/VC framework for full credential lifecycle management
specific.detail: Implement a user-centric DID/VC framework enforcing secure lifecycle synchronization, including enrollment, verification, re-issuance, and revocation, and test its effectiveness in mitigating biometric leakage while ensuring authorized access.

specific.short: Evaluate overall system performance, resilience & consensus integrity
specific.detail: Evaluate the system's consensus integrity and operational resilience across distributed edge validators, assessing the combined effectiveness of cancelable techniques, blockchain integration, and user-centric identity management.
